questions.question: The Government has approved an Electronics Manufacturing Cluster (EMC) in which city?
questions.explanation: The government has approved an Electronics Manufacturing Cluster (EMC) in Pune at a cost of Rs 500 crore. The proposal, approved by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology, is expected to attract investments of up to Rs 2,000 crore. The cluster will be set up over an area of 297 acres.

questions.question: Which Indian state/UT has successfully set up ‘Bio-Villages’?
questions.explanation: Tripura government has successfully set up five ‘Bio-Villages’ for promoting sustainable development, climate change mitigation and climate adaption. As per the Compendium of Best Practice on Climate Action from Indian states, in Tripura, over 500 households have been benefited with the successful implementation in five ‘Bio-Villages’. The concept of ‘Bio-Village’ was introduced by the Directorate of Biotechnology, Tripura in 2018 for promoting organic farming.

questions.question: SPARK is a Studentship program recently launched in which field?
questions.explanation: The Central Council for Research in Ayurvedic Sciences (CCRAS) launched the Studentship Program for Ayurveda Research Ken (SPARK). It was launched for Ayurveda (BAMS) students studying in recognised Ayurveda colleges. The selected research fellows will be offered a financial support of Rs.50,000 under the fellowship.

questions.question: Who is the head of the ‘Committee for drafting of the national cooperative policy document’?
questions.explanation: The Union Cooperative Ministry announced the constitution of a committee for drafting of the national cooperative policy document. The 47-member committee will be headed by former Union Minister Suresh Prabhu. The existing National Policy on Cooperatives was formulated in 2002. At present, India has around 8.5 lakh co-operative societies with a member base of around 29 crore.

questions.question: Which bank has unveiled its ‘Bank on Wheels’ service for Gujarat?
questions.explanation: HDFC Bank unveiled its state-of-the art ‘Bank on Wheels’ service, for the state of Gujarat. It is part of a pilot project to offer banking services in unbanked locations. The Rural Banking segment of HDFC bank has introduced ‘Bank on Wheels’ vans which will visit remote villages located 10 – 25 km from the nearest branch. The van will cover 3 villages in a day and customers can access 21 banking products and services.

questions.question: Which city is the host of ‘National Conference on agriculture for Rabi campaign 2022-23’
questions.explanation: The National Conference on agriculture for Rabi campaign 2022-23 was inaugurated by the Union Agriculture Minister Narendra Singh Tomar. It included discussions on crops, prospects of further progress, challenges, technologies, solutions and government’s plans and processes. The production of food grains in the country is estimated at 3157 lakh tones, which is higher by 50 lakh tonnes than 2020-21.

questions.question: Which Union Ministry organised the ‘EU-India Green Hydrogen Forum’?

options.label: B
options.text: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy

answer.label: B
answer.text: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy

questions.explanation: The first EU-India Green Hydrogen Forum was organized jointly by the Delegation of the European Union and the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy. It was launched in cooperation with the Confederation of Indian Industry (CII) and Hydrogen Europe. The event brought together EU-India businesses to discuss joint projects on hydrogen and international hydrogen trade.

questions.question: India recently pulled out of which pillar of the Indo-Pacific Economic Framework (IPEF)?
questions.explanation: India pulled out of the trade-related negotiations of the United States-led Indo-Pacific Economic Framework (IPEF). Union Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal confirmed India’s decision of not joining the trade pillar of IPEF. The IPEF Ministerial Meet was recently hosted by the United States.

questions.question: Who was recently named as the Monarch in Australia and New Zealand?
questions.explanation: King Charles III was officially named monarch in Australia and New Zealand, in tandem ceremonies marking his ascension to the throne. King Charles III has been proclaimed head of state of both Australia and New Zealand at ceremonies in the nations’ capitals.

questions.question: Which country hosted the ‘Exercise Pitch Black 22’ multilateral defence exercise?
questions.explanation: Exercise Pitch Black 22 was hosted by the Royal Australian Air Force for a duration of three weeks. It included participation of 17 Air Forces and over 2500 military personnel. The Indian Air Force contingent, which also participated in the multinational exercise, included four Su-30 MKI & two C-17 aircraft.
